title: haplopappus paucidentatus
intro: Haplopappus paucidentatus, also known as the few-toothed rabbitbrush or snowberry rabbitbrush, is a shrub species in the daisy family native to western North America. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits associated with Haplopappus paucidentatus include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and act as an antiseptic to aid in wound healing. Haplopappus paucidentatus is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is used in herbal remedies to promote overall well-being and support the immune system.
